title: Build v0 of the Python utility (src/ + smoke test)
description:
Current time: 11:00. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Your mission
Pick a useful domain and build a small Python utility under workspace/src/. Target: under 200 lines, standard library only (or one well-justified third-party package). The utility must be **offline-friendly** — no network calls during normal operation.

## Suggested domain (your call)
A **log file analyser** — reads one or more log files, counts lines by log level (INFO / WARNING / ERROR / CRITICAL), shows a summary table, and can filter/search by level or keyword. Pure stdlib (argparse, re, collections, pathlib). Compact and genuinely useful.

## Deliverables
1. `workspace/src/logalyzer.py` — the main utility (≤ 200 lines).
2. `workspace/src/test_smoke.py` — smoke test that actually runs (`python test_smoke.py` exits 0). Test with a small inline log string written to a temp file; no network.
3. (Optional) `workspace/src/__init__.py` if needed.

## Before marking complete
- Run `python workspace/src/test_smoke.py` locally and confirm it exits 0.
- Check line count: `wc -l workspace/src/logalyzer.py` must be ≤ 200.
- Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md in workspace/ before submitting.

title: Write workspace/README.md for the utility
description:
Current time: 11:00. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Your mission
Write workspace/README.md for the Python log-analyser utility built by research_engineer. Read the source files under workspace/src/ before writing.

## README must include
1. **Project title & one-line description**
2. **Features** — bullet list of what the tool does
3. **Requirements** — Python version, stdlib-only (or named package if used)
4. **Installation** — how to get the code (clone instructions or just "no install needed")
5. **Usage** — CLI examples with realistic sample output (invent plausible output if needed)
6. **Running the tests** — exact command to run the smoke test
7. **License** — MIT

## Constraints
- File goes at workspace/README.md (NOT inside src/).
- Write for a technical audience (developers / sysadmins).
- No marketing fluff. Clear, concise, accurate.
- Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md in workspace/ before submitting.

title: Review v0 — write workspace/reviews/review_v1.md
description:
Current time: 11:00. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Your mission
Review every code artifact under workspace/src/. Write your findings to workspace/reviews/review_v1.md.

## What to inspect
1. `workspace/src/logalyzer.py` — correctness, edge cases, security (no shell injection, no eval, no hardcoded secrets), style, line count ≤ 200.
2. `workspace/src/test_smoke.py` — does it actually test something meaningful? Would it catch a broken implementation?
3. Cross-check against workspace/DONE_CRITERIA.md.

## Review format (write exactly this structure)
```
# Code Review — v1

## Verdict: APPROVE | REVISE

## Summary
<2-3 sentences overall>

## Blocking issues (REVISE only)
- <issue 1 — be specific, quote the line/function>
- <issue 2>
...

## Non-blocking suggestions
- <suggestion>

## Line count check
logalyzer.py: <N> lines — PASS / FAIL
```

## Rules
- Verdict is APPROVE only if there are ZERO blocking issues.
- List every blocking issue explicitly — the engineer will fix exactly what you list.
- Be specific: quote function names, line numbers, or code snippets.
- Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md in workspace/ before submitting.

note: 195-line log analyser + smoke test. All 17 tests pass, exit 0. stdlib only (argparse, re, collections, pathlib). Offline, no network calls. Smoke test covers classify_line, analyse_file (no filter, level filter, keyword, combined), and main() exit codes + flags.

README written after reading logalyzer.py and test_smoke.py. Covers all six required sections: title/description, features, requirements, installation, usage (four CLI examples with plausible output), test command, and MIT license. Caveats section added for encoding, first-match-wins classification, and no tail-mode.

title: Revise v0 — fix blocking issue from review_v1.md
description:
Current time: 11:40. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Context
review_v1.md verdict: REVISE. You must fix the blocking issue below before this task is complete. The non-blocking suggestions are also listed — address them if you can do so cleanly.

---

## BLOCKING ISSUE (must fix — quoted verbatim from review_v1.md)

> **README.md:88–102 vs logalyzer.py:66** — The `--level ERROR --show-lines` example
> in the README shows a summary table that contains *only* `ERROR  15  100.0%`.
> In the actual implementation, `counts[level] += 1` (line 66) executes for every
> line **before** the filter check at lines 68–74, so `counts` always reflects the
> full file distribution regardless of `--level`. Running the same command against
> a real file will display all levels (INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL, UNKNOWN) in
> the summary — not just ERROR at 100%. Either:
> (a) fix the code so that `counts` is gated by `level_filter` (making the summary
>     show only the requested level), or
> (b) correct the README example to match the actual all-levels summary output.
> As written, the deliverable ships with documentation that actively misleads users
> about a core feature.

Pick **one** path (a or b) and apply it consistently. If you choose (a), update `src/logalyzer.py` and leave a comment in the README for partnerships_analyst to update the example. If you choose (b), update `README.md` directly.

---

## NON-BLOCKING SUGGESTIONS (fix if clean, skip if risky)

1. > **logalyzer.py:43–47 and 81–87** — `show_lines: bool = False` is accepted by
   > both `analyse_file` and `analyse_files` but is never read inside either function
   > body. Remove it from both signatures (or use it).

2. > **logalyzer.py:168–171** — `main()` does not validate `args.level` against the
   > known canonical set. A user typo like `--level ERRO` produces an empty matched
   > list with no diagnostic. Consider checking `args.level.upper() in LEVELS_ORDER`
   > and printing a helpful error before returning 1.

3. > **test_smoke.py:34** — `FAILURES: list[str] = []` is a module-level mutable list.
   > If `main_runner()` were ever called more than once in the same process, failures
   > would accumulate. Move the list inside `main_runner()` or reset it at entry.

---

## Before marking complete
- Run `python src/test_smoke.py` and confirm it exits 0.
- Line count `wc -l src/logalyzer.py` must still be ≤ 200.
- Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md before submitting.

title: Update README.md to match revised code (v1)
description:
Current time: 11:40. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Context
research_engineer just revised logalyzer.py and/or README.md to fix a mismatch between the `--level ERROR --show-lines` example and the actual summary-table output. Read `src/logalyzer.py` carefully to understand the current behaviour, then update `README.md` so every example is accurate.

## Specifically check
- The `--level ERROR --show-lines` example (README.md lines 88–102): does the summary table shown match what the code actually produces? Fix it if not.
- Any other examples — verify they are plausible given the current implementation.
- Do NOT change the structure, tone, or any section that is already correct.

## Constraint
File lives at `workspace/README.md` (root of workspace, not inside src/).
Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md before submitting.

title: Review v1 — write workspace/reviews/review_v2.md
description:
Current time: 11:40. Session ends at 4:00pm.

## Context
This is review round 2 of max 4. research_engineer addressed the blocking issue from review_v1.md and the non-blocking suggestions. partnerships_analyst updated README.md.

## What to inspect
1. `src/logalyzer.py` — confirm the blocking issue from v1 is fixed: does `--level LEVEL` now gate the summary-table counts, OR has the README example been corrected to match the actual all-levels output? One of those must be true. Also check the non-blocking fixes were applied cleanly.
2. `src/test_smoke.py` — does the test still pass (check for obvious regressions)? If the count-gating fix was applied, is there a test for the new behaviour?
3. `README.md` — are all examples now accurate against the actual code?
4. Cross-check DONE_CRITERIA.md.

## Review format (write exactly this structure)
```
# Code Review — v2

## Verdict: APPROVE | REVISE

## Summary
<2-3 sentences overall>

## Blocking issues (REVISE only)
- <issue — specific, quote line/function>

## Non-blocking suggestions
- <suggestion>

## Line count check
logalyzer.py: <N> lines — PASS / FAIL
```

Verdict is APPROVE only if there are ZERO blocking issues.
Re-read DONE_CRITERIA.md before submitting.

note: All review_v1.md issues addressed. Blocking: README --level example corrected (path b) to show full distribution summary + filtered lines. Non-blocking 1: removed dead show_lines param from analyse_file/analyse_files. Non-blocking 2: added --level validation with helpful error message. Non-blocking 3: FAILURES.clear() at main_runner() entry. 19 tests pass, exit 0. 197 lines.
Fixed four arithmetic/formatting errors in the examples. No structural or tonal changes made.
